title: Throttle daily podcast retries on upstream writer failures
description: Add bounded retry backoff for both fp-digest and the-rundown jobs so intermittent upstream LLM failures do not hammer Anthropic/opencode every 10 seconds. Reuse existing process_after scheduling and keep collection reuse behavior unchanged.
close_reason: Both daily podcast jobs now use bounded retry backoff via process_after, with failure state tracked in the DB and consumer retry logs showing next scheduled attempt.

title: Preserve publish-script source artifacts and produce dog cancer vaccine deep-dive
description: Preserve source script and show-notes artifacts for future publish-script/deep-dives episodes, and use that capability to produce a new deep-dives-style episode about the dog cancer vaccine story. Current implementation path: pipeline/script_processor.py plus the publish-script CLI in pipeline/__main__.py. Existing limitation: published metadata is stored, but the source script/show-notes artifacts are not persisted under /persist/my-podcasts/scripts, which blocks reliable re-publishing, auditing, and follow-on deep dives. Expected follow-up work should add durable persistence for those source artifacts, confirm it integrates with the deep-dives feed that already exists, and create/publish the dog cancer vaccine episode using the preserved-artifact workflow.
notes: Starting point for future handoff: investigate how pipeline/script_processor.py and the publish-script entrypoint in pipeline/__main__.py handle incoming script/show-notes inputs, then add persistence of those source artifacts under /persist/my-podcasts/scripts alongside existing published metadata. Second deliverable is a new deep-dives-style episode for the dog cancer vaccine story, published via the existing deep-dives feed using the new artifact-preservation path.
close_reason: Implemented auto-archival of source scripts for publish-script episodes and published dog cancer deep-dive to deep-dives feed. Verified: 16/16 tests pass, DB row present, archived files in /persist/my-podcasts/scripts/deep-dives/, live feed at podcast.mohrbacher.dev/feeds/deep-dives.xml shows both episodes.

title: Document daily podcast hardening and incident response
description: Add repo docs for the daily podcast hardening work: top-level AGENTS TOC updates, pipeline-specific AGENTS quick start, and operational skills covering monitoring, retries, empty-output hardening, and current manual recovery flow.
close_reason: Added AGENTS navigation, pipeline runbook, and skills for daily podcast hardening and incident response.

title: Add admin CLI to inspect and reset errored daily jobs
description: Add a small jobs CLI surface for listing errored fp-digest / the-rundown jobs and resetting them back to pending, clearing stale work artifacts by default. Follow up by documenting the operational flow in a repo skill.
close_reason: Completed admin CLI for listing and resetting errored daily podcast jobs, plus operator skill and AGENTS.md discovery docs

title: Fix FP digest empty writer output retries
close_reason: Writer-stage validation now rejects empty FP output before TTS, with 300s timeout and regression coverage.

title: Cap daily podcast retries and mark exhausted jobs errored
description: Add a max retry limit for fp-digest and the-rundown jobs so prolonged upstream outages stop retrying forever. After the cap, mark the job errored, keep the last error, and stop scheduling new attempts until manually reset or requeued.
close_reason: Daily podcast retries now stop after the 12-hour retry budget is exhausted and jobs are marked errored with the final failure recorded.

title: Refactor source-specific processing with adapters and add Yglesias title format
description: Refactor pipeline source-specific branching into a maintainable adapter architecture. Current processor behavior includes multiple feed-specific conditionals for Levine and Yglesias (title formatting, body cleanup, source URL extraction). Implement a pluggable source adapter layer and migrate existing behaviors into adapters. Include requested Yglesias title format: 'YYYY-MM-DD - Slow Boring - <Title>'.
acceptance_criteria: Core processor has no feed_slug if/elif branches for source-specific logic; Levine and Yglesias behaviors are preserved or improved; New Yglesias title format is active for newly processed episodes; Tests cover adapter behaviors and regressions.
notes: Refactor shipped end-to-end. Core processor no longer has feed_slug if/elif branches for source-specific logic.

title: Define source adapter interface and wiring points in processor
description: Design a small source adapter contract for newsletter-specific behavior. Proposed methods: format_title(date_str, subject_raw, subject_slug), clean_body(raw_email, body), extract_source_url(raw_email, date_str, subject_raw), and optional metadata hooks. Document where the adapter is resolved and invoked in process_email_bytes.
acceptance_criteria: Adapter interface documented in code comments/design notes; processor wiring points identified; no behavior changes yet.
notes: Completed: introduced source adapter contract and registry in pipeline/source_adapters.py; processor wiring now resolves adapter by feed_slug and delegates title, body cleanup, and source URL extraction.

title: Implement adapter registry and migrate Levine/Yglesias logic out of processor conditionals
description: Create source adapter modules (default, levine, yglesias), register by feed_slug/preset key, and move source-specific logic from process_email_bytes into adapter implementations. Keep behavior parity for existing Levine URL extraction and Yglesias body cleanup/source URL extraction.
acceptance_criteria: process_email_bytes delegates source-specific work to adapters; existing output parity validated on representative sample emails.
notes: Completed: migrated Levine and Yglesias source-specific logic out of processor conditionals into LevineAdapter and YglesiasAdapter.

title: Add Yglesias title format: date + Slow Boring + post title
description: Implement Yglesias-specific title formatter in the adapter so new episodes are titled 'YYYY-MM-DD - Slow Boring - <Title>'. Ensure raw subject normalization strips newsletter prefixes where needed while preserving meaningful punctuation/casing.
acceptance_criteria: New Yglesias episodes use requested title format; Levine title behavior unchanged; unit tests cover formatting edge cases.
notes: Completed: Yglesias title format now 'YYYY-MM-DD - Slow Boring - <Title>' for new episodes via YglesiasAdapter.format_title.

title: Regression tests, rollout verification, and docs update for adapter refactor
description: Expand tests to cover adapter dispatch and source behaviors (Levine and Yglesias). Run full suite and lint. Verify one live newsletter per source post-refactor to ensure queue->audio->feed path still works. Update AGENTS/skill docs if operational behavior changes.
acceptance_criteria: Tests pass; no regression in feed/audio outputs; docs updated for any changed operator expectations.
notes: Completed: updated tests for adapter behavior and source URL/cleanup; uv run ruff check pipeline and uv run pytest both passing (24 tests). Restarted my-podcasts-consumer.

title: Harden Rundown writer against empty LLM output
description: Add the same empty-script guard used in fp_writer to rundown_writer so zero-byte writer output cannot silently reach downstream stages if the LLM returns only tags/whitespace.
close_reason: Rundown writer now rejects empty LLM output before downstream processing, mirroring FP hardening.
